what_changed: Provider-side imported literal export safety now admits JS/TS new Map(...) only through the existing JsLikeMapConstructor LibraryApi occurrence proof.
Provider-side imported literal export safety now admits JS/TS new Set(...) only through the existing JsLikeSetConstructor LibraryApi occurrence proof.

The imported snapshot copies provider constructor evidence into the importer with provider source-origin spans and rewired dependency ids, so importer-local names cannot shadow provider-proven Map/Set globals.
Plain Map(...)/Set(...), provider-local constructor shadowing, provider/importer mutations, wrong contents, missing LibraryApi evidence, and raw import-coordinate sequences remain closed.

notes: Missing JS/TS constructor LibraryApi evidence blocks snapshots.
The full crates recall-loss report did not move oracle completeness because the new capability is pinned by focused imported-value fixtures rather than an existing crates corpus under-merge.

The +2 admission rejections come from additional Rust test/helper units introduced by this change; they land in the structured callee-identity bucket, with no new top opportunities and no hard-gate movement.
This phase strengthens the semantic kernel by reusing existing constructor LibraryApi capabilities across the import-snapshot boundary instead of adding selector-specific API exceptions.
decision.follow_up: Phase 2 should generalize the same imported immutable provider-snapshot lane to more existing collection/map factory contracts, starting with Java collection factories and Ruby require-backed Set providers. Phase 3 should add recall-loss/reporting instrumentation for import-snapshot miss reasons so corpus gaps become visible without adding one-off APIs.
